label: PASS
rationale: The trajectory has ReEncryptState ACTIVE followed by a successful PAUSE_req. GeneralStatus is valid for the resulting PAUSED/PENDING state, and gen_status value 3 is active_pause_requested for last state ACTIVE with PAUSE_req detected, so returning column 19 value 3 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The trajectory has ReEncryptState ACTIVE followed by a successful PAUSE_req. Table 87 maps this condition to GeneralStatus value 3 (active_pause_requested), while value 4 is for PENDING plus PAUSE_req, so returning column 19 value 4 is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The trajectory has ReEncryptState PENDING followed by a successful PAUSE_req. GeneralStatus is valid for PAUSED/PENDING, and gen_status value 4 is pend_pause_requested for last state PENDING with PAUSE_req detected, so returning column 19 value 4 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The trajectory has ReEncryptState PENDING followed by a successful PAUSE_req. Table 87 maps this condition to GeneralStatus value 4 (pend_pause_requested), while value 3 is for ACTIVE plus PAUSE_req, so returning column 19 value 3 is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: START_req is accepted from IDLE and changes ReEncryptState to PENDING. LastReEncryptLBA is valid in PENDING and must be 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF when no LBA has been successfully re-encrypted, so returning the all-ones sentinel in column 17 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: START_req from IDLE moves the range to PENDING. With no successful LBA re-encryption shown, LastReEncryptLBA must be the all-ones sentinel; returning column 17 value 0 incorrectly reports LBA 0 as the last good re-encrypted LBA.
